title: bromus patulus var. vestitus
intro: Bromus patulus var. vestitus, also known as the fescue grass or a type of wild brome, is a plant species in the Poaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Bromus patulus var. vestitus include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea, or made into a poultice to apply topically for its therapeutic benefits.
